_comment: CocoShaMem seed — the starting shared memories. Each memory has 'labels' (security labels it CARRIES — who may see it; the Bouncer requires the user's clearances to cover ALL of them) and 'topics' (what it is ABOUT — relevance/search, ANY overlap with the query matches). The settlement memory carries TWO labels: only a user holding BOTH financials AND legal can see it. LINEAGE: 'm-briefing' is a DERIVED memory — 'derived_from' names its sources and its labels are the UNION of theirs (financials+legal), so it inherits both sources' access constraints (only ben-auditor holds both); revoking a source id in data/lineage.json hides the briefing too (bouncer.revoked_ids). JSON has no comments, so this '_comment' key is ignored.

memories.text: The deli on Carter Lane does great sandwiches.
Visitor parking is on Friar Street.
The Watford depot accepts deliveries only before 11am.
Q3 revenue for the London office was 4.2M.
The Acme supplier contract has a late-delivery penalty clause.
The Acme dispute settled for 250k — commercially confidential.
Risk briefing: Q3 London revenue was 4.2M and the Acme contract carries a late-delivery penalty.
All deliveries are cancelled due to road closures outside the warehouse, until further notice.
